hero.tagline: From Chandni Chowk to Connaught Place — Delhi's streets have fed legends for centuries.
highlights: 17 iconic food spots across Old & New Delhi
Home of India's most famous Chole Bhature
Street food trails from Red Fort to Kamla Nagar

foodSpots.description: Famous Old Delhi street breakfast where spicy chole are served with soft kulche topped with ginger, coriander and tangy chutney. Known for its strong spicy gravy that wakes you up in the morning.
foodSpots.tips: Best for early morning breakfast. Visit between 7–10 AM when the chole are freshly prepared.
foodSpots.routeDetails: From Red Fort walk or take an e-rickshaw towards Chawri Bazaar market. The stall is located in the busy street food lane of Old Delhi.

foodSpots.description: Thick Punjabi style stuffed parathas cooked on a tawa with generous butter. Crispy outside with soft potato filling inside.
foodSpots.tips: Perfect North Indian breakfast. Best enjoyed with butter and chai during winter mornings.
foodSpots.routeDetails: Take an auto from Majnu Ka Tilla or Delhi University area to Ashok Vihar Phase-1 market where this famous paratha shop is located.

foodSpots.description: One of Delhi's most famous chole bhature spots. Soft fluffy bhature served with rich velvety chole and tangy pickle.
foodSpots.tips: Very popular breakfast place. Visit before noon to avoid long queues. Pair with lassi.
foodSpots.routeDetails: From Connaught Place take a short auto ride or walk towards Paharganj market near the New Delhi Railway Station side.

foodSpots.description: Crispy Amritsari style kulcha stuffed with spicy potatoes and served with mild chole and tangy chutney.
foodSpots.tips: Heavy Punjabi breakfast. Best eaten fresh with lassi for a full meal.
foodSpots.routeDetails: Take an auto or cab from Janakpuri area to Vikaspuri market where this kulcha shop is located.

foodSpots.description: Traditional Old Delhi breakfast of spicy bedmi puri served with potato curry and nagori halwa. A sweet-spicy combination loved by locals.
foodSpots.tips: Classic Old Delhi Sunday breakfast. Best before 11 AM when halwa is freshly prepared.
foodSpots.routeDetails: From Chandni Chowk or Red Fort take a short walk or rickshaw ride to Chawri Bazaar where Shyam Sweets is located.

foodSpots.description: Unique breakfast where fresh white butter is churned in the morning and served on toast with spices or sugar.
foodSpots.tips: Light morning snack. Try early morning when fresh butter is prepared.
foodSpots.routeDetails: From Akshardham take metro or cab towards Shahdara Chhota Bazaar area near Baburam School.

foodSpots.description: Thick spicy chole served with paneer stuffed bhature and chutney. Known for its rich Punjabi masala flavor.
foodSpots.tips: Heavy Punjabi breakfast or brunch meal. Perfect after visiting Adventure Island.
foodSpots.routeDetails: From Rohini West metro or Adventure Island take an auto to the nearby market where Gopal Ji shop is located.

foodSpots.description: Traditional North Indian sweet with crispy outer layer and soft sugary inside. Famous morning dessert in Shahdara.
foodSpots.tips: Perfect sweet after breakfast. Best when served warm and fresh.
foodSpots.routeDetails: Take metro or cab towards Shahdara Chhota Bazaar market where the sweet shop is located.

foodSpots.description: Mild spiced chole served with crispy puri and halwa. A popular student breakfast in Kamla Nagar.
foodSpots.tips: Popular among college students. Good budget breakfast near DU campus.
foodSpots.routeDetails: Walk from Delhi University North Campus towards Kamla Nagar market food street.

foodSpots.description: Famous West Delhi breakfast spot serving semi dry chole with fluffy bhature and kulhad lassi.
foodSpots.tips: Great breakfast place after shopping in Rajouri Garden. Try kulhad lassi.
foodSpots.routeDetails: From Rajouri Garden metro station walk or take auto towards Prem Di Hatti near the market area.

foodSpots.description: Thick rich chole with fluffy paneer bhature served with chutney and pickle. Considered among the best chole bhature spots in Delhi.
foodSpots.tips: Perfect breakfast or brunch. Visit before 12 PM to avoid long queues.
foodSpots.routeDetails: From New Delhi Railway Station Paharganj side walk a few minutes to reach Radhe Shyam shop.

foodSpots.distanceFromTouristPlace: ~200 m from Defence Colony Market main road
foodSpots.description: Delhi style shawarma roll filled with lightly spiced chicken, onion, mayo, coriander and spring onion wrapped in soft flatbread. Served with garlic mint chutney.
foodSpots.tips: Perfect evening snack or quick dinner. Expect rush after 7 PM.
foodSpots.routeDetails: Enter Defence Colony Market from the main gate and walk towards the food lane where Concept Shawarma is located.

foodSpots.distanceFromTouristPlace: ~300 m from Kamla Nagar Market gate
foodSpots.description: Crispy urad dal stuffed kachoris served with spicy potato gravy and methi chutney. Thin crunchy texture makes it a classic Delhi breakfast snack.
foodSpots.tips: Best morning street breakfast. Very spicy so keep water ready.
foodSpots.routeDetails: Walk inside Kamla Nagar market street food lane near clothing shops to reach this small shop.

foodSpots.distanceFromTouristPlace: ~350 m from Kamla Nagar main market
foodSpots.description: Double fried bread pakoda stuffed with potato and paneer filling served uniquely with chole gravy, alongside traditional kalakand made from thickened milk topped with pistachios and almonds.
foodSpots.tips: Great evening snack with tea. Often crowded during evening hours. Fresh kalakand available in the afternoon.
foodSpots.fullAddress: Near Sharma Ji Kachori, Kamla Nagar, Delhi
foodSpots.routeDetails: From Kamla Nagar main market walk towards the snack street next to Sharma Ji Kachori.

foodSpots.distanceFromTouristPlace: ~400 m from Kamla Nagar market gate
foodSpots.description: Crispy potato patties stuffed with paneer, lentils and nuts topped with sweet, sour and spicy chutneys including special kachalu chutney.
foodSpots.tips: Best evening street food snack. Heavy enough to replace dinner.
foodSpots.routeDetails: Walk inside Kamla Nagar chaat street where Vaishnav Chaat Bhandar is located.

foodSpots.distanceFromTouristPlace: ~500 m from Dwarka Sector 7 Metro Station
foodSpots.description: Creamy Afghani style soya chaap cooked in butter, cream and mild spices alongside classic Delhi butter chicken in thick creamy tomato gravy with kasuri methi. Both served with tandoori roti or butter naan.
foodSpots.tips: Best dinner spot for both vegetarians and non-vegetarians. Try with butter naan or rumali roti.
foodSpots.routeDetails: From Dwarka Sector 7 metro station walk or take a short auto ride to the market where the restaurant is located.

foodSpots.description: Fried chicken momos cooked in creamy onion capsicum gravy with butter and cream. A fusion Delhi style momo dish.
foodSpots.tips: Great evening snack or dinner. Best enjoyed hot with spicy momo chutney.
foodSpots.routeDetails: From Dwarka metro take a short auto ride to KC Restaurant located in the local market area.
